title: 7 Herbal Creams For Urinary Calculus
intro_desc: Herbal creams for urinary calculus are becoming increasingly popular as a natural solution to alleviate the symptoms of kidney stones. These creams are often made from herbal teas that have been found to relieve urinary calculus, which is the formation of stones in the urinary tract. One reason herbal teas can be beneficial is that they contain compounds that help to break down and dissolve the stones, making it easier to pass them through urine.  For instance, Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and prevent the formation of new stones. Urtica dioica, or stinging nettle, is another herb that has been found to have diuretic properties, which helps to increase urine production and flush out the stones. Curcuma longa, or turmeric, contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with urinary calculus.  Drinking herbal teas made from these herbs can bring several benefits to your life. Not only can they help to alleviate the symptoms of urinary calculus, but they can also help to prevent the formation of new stones. Additionally, herbal teas can be a natural and non-invasive way to treat urinary calculus, reducing the need for surgery or other medical interventions. By incorporating herbal teas into your daily routine, you may be able to maintain a healthy urinary tract and prevent the formation of kidney stones.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Glycyrrhizin also has a diuretic effect, increasing urine production and helping to flush out the kidneys and urinary tract. The licorice extract in these creams also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help protect the kidneys from damage. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been found to inhibit the growth of certain types of bacteria that can contribute to urinary tract infections and the formation of urinary calculi. By reducing inflammation and promoting urine production, glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help to prevent the formation of urinary calculi and alleviate symptoms associated with kidney stones.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Heat the distilled water in a saucepan over low heat until it's warm.
Add the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the warm water and stir well. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a separate container and discard the solids. Add the beeswax, coconut oil, and vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool until it solidifies. Store the Glycyrrhiza glabra cream in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams contains the bioactive constituents histamine, acetylcholine, and polysaccharides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort caused by urinary calculus, or kidney stones. The polysaccharides in Urtica dioica creams have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria that can contribute to the formation of kidney stones. The diuretic properties of histamine and acetylcholine help increase urine production, which can help flush out kidney stones and reduce the risk of new stones forming. By reducing inflammation and promoting urine production, Urtica dioica creams may help alleviate symptoms and promote recovery in individuals with urinary calculus.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal creams for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Urtica dioica leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Dry the Urtica dioica leaves in a low-temperature oven (150°F - 200°F) for 1-2 hours. Then, grind the leaves into a fine powder.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until the beeswax is fully melted.
Add the ground Urtica dioica powder, shea butter, and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency similar to thin honey. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains bioactive compounds like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. Curcumin specifically has been shown to inhibit the formation of kidney stones by reducing the concentration of oxalate, a key component of urinary calculi, in the urine. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help protect the kidneys from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the formation of kidney stones. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and inhibiting the formation of oxalate, Curcuma longa creams may provide relief from urinary calculus symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Mix 2 cups of distilled water with 1 cup of beeswax in a double boiler. Heat until the beeswax melts.
Add 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder to the melted beeswax. Stir well.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 10 minutes. It should start to thicken.
Add 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel to the mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once set, it's ready to use as a cream for urinary calculus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains several bioactive constituents, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help reduce swelling and inflammation in the urinary tract, making it easier for the body to pass kidney stones or urinary calculi. The gel also contains mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, the aloe's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce the formation of kidney stones by preventing the formation of calcium oxalate crystals, a common component of urinary calculi. By reducing inflammation and soothing the urinary tract, aloe barbadensis creams may provide relief from the pain and discomfort associated with kidney stones.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval.
Once melted, add the Aloe barbadensis gel and shea butter to the mixture. Stir until well combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches a temperature of around 120°F to 130°F.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before use. Store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains bioactive constituents like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation and ease pain in the urinary tract, making it easier to pass stones. The diuretic properties of gingerols also increase urine production, which can help flush out small stones and prevent new ones from forming. Additionally, shogaols have antimicrobial properties that prevent bacterial growth in the urinary tract, which can help prevent infections that can lead to the formation of stones. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, Zingiber officinale creams can help alleviate symptoms and promote the passage of urinary calculi.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Zingiber officinale roots, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Grind the dried Zingiber officinale roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add 2 tablespoons of the ground Zingiber officinale powder to the melted oil and wax, and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean container, let it cool and solidify, and your Zingiber officinale cream is ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale creams contains flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, and sesquiterpene lactones, including taraxasterol and taraxol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract. The sesquiterpene lactones in Taraxacum officinale creams have also been shown to have diuretic properties, which can help increase urine production and flush out kidney stones. Additionally, the flavonoids in the cream have antioxidant properties that can help protect the kidneys from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and increasing urine production, Taraxacum officinale creams may help prevent the formation of new kidney stones and alleviate symptoms associated with urinary calculus.
remedies.image_alt: Taraxacum officinale herbal creams for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves, 2 cups of water, and a cheesecloth.
Combine the Taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves with the water in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through the cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Combine 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring until the beeswax melts.
Combine the cooled Taraxacum officinale infusion with the melted beeswax mixture. Stir well and pour the mixture into a container. Let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense creams contains silicic acid, flavonoids, and saponins. These constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and damage in the urinary tract. The saponins in Equisetum arvense creams also have a diuretic effect, increasing urine production and helping to flush out small stones and gravel. The flavonoids have a protective effect on the kidneys and bladder, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. By reducing inflammation and promoting urine production, Equisetum arvense creams may help prevent the formation of new stones and alleviate symptoms of urinary calculus.
remedies.image_alt: Equisetum arvense herbal creams for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Equisetum arvense leaves, 1 cup of coconut oil, and a double boiler.
Chop the Equisetum arvense leaves into small pieces and add to the double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil in the double boiler and heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let it cool slightly before straining the mixture through a cheesecloth.
Transfer the strained mixture to a clean container and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent urinary calculus, also known as kidney stones, from forming. Certain herbs like juniper berries, uva ursi, and marshmallow root have been traditionally used to help prevent and pass stones. They may help by increasing urine flow and reducing stone formation.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for urinary calculus daily can be beneficial, but it's essential to be cautious. Some herbs like juniper berries, uva ursi, and marshmallow root may cause issues if taken excessively or long-term. It's best to drink them in moderation, about 1-2 cups a day, to avoid potential side effects.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can take a few weeks to show results in treating urinary calculus. They work by dissolving the kidney stones and helping them pass out of the body. Some people may see improvement within a week, but it's common for it to take two to four weeks for noticeable results. The effectiveness varies from person to person.
faq_time: For urinary calculus, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning. This allows it to work all day, helping to break down stones and prevent new ones from forming. Drinking it in the morning gives it a head start on doing its job, so it's more effective throughout the day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for urinary calculus. For example, if you're taking diuretics, herbal teas like juniper or uva ursi might increase their effect and worsen dehydration. Similarly, if you're taking medications to prevent kidney stones, certain herbal teas like stinging nettle could interfere with their absorption.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have urinary calculus, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some teas, like those with juniper or pennyroyal, can cause problems for you and your baby. It's also not clear if other herbal teas can help or hurt. It's usually a good idea to avoid them until your baby is born.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas, especially for urinary issues like kidney stones. Some herbal teas can pass into breast milk and potentially affect the baby. If you're trying herbal teas for urinary calculus, consider the tea's ingredients and potential impact on your baby. Always monitor your baby's behavior after drinking the tea.
